title: BEM Naming Grammar
purpose: Define the class-name grammar used by the BEM methodology.
scope: This specification covers block, element, and modifier name forms plus the negative rules that keep those forms unambiguous. It does not define CSS selectors or layout behavior.
context: The naming grammar is the part most teams implement first, so it needs concrete syntax rules instead of a vague style preference. The grammar also has to stay aligned with the shared terms in `SPEC-BEM-CONCEPTS` so the namespace rules remain meaningful.

requirements.title: Match block names to the block-name pattern
notes: The pattern keeps block names lowercase, hyphen-separated, and free of separator collisions.

requirements.title: Use block names as namespaces
requirements.statement: A block name MUST define the namespace for its elements and modifiers.
notes: The namespace is the `block-name` prefix that anchors `block-name__element-name` and `block-name_modifier-name` forms.

requirements.title: Use double underscores for element names
requirements.statement: An element name MUST use the `block-name__element-name` form.

requirements.title: Forbid element-of-element names
requirements.statement: An element name MUST NOT use nested element separators such as `block__elem1__elem2`.

requirements.title: Use single underscores for modifiers
requirements.statement: A modifier name MUST use the `block-name_modifier-name` or `block-name__element-name_modifier-name` form.

requirements.title: Require a keyed modifier value suffix
requirements.statement: A keyed block or element modifier name MUST add a `_modifier-value` suffix.

requirements.title: Omit the modifier value for Boolean modifiers
requirements.statement: A Boolean block or element modifier MUST omit the modifier value.
notes: The presence of the class is the signal for a Boolean modifier.

requirements.title: Forbid standalone modifiers
requirements.statement: A block or element modifier MUST NOT be used without its modified block or element.

requirements.title: Exclude separator characters from block names
requirements.statement: A block name MUST NOT contain `__` or `_` separators.
notes: Excluding separator characters keeps the block namespace unambiguous.
